proven.claim: PROVEN: applyP2 called once with N contracts finds N-1 adjacent pairs per function;
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 41
; PROVEN: applyP2 called once with N contracts finds N-1 adjacent pairs per function;
; calling it per-contract (with a single-element slice) yields 0 pairs.
; Code transition: byFunction groups contracts by function key.
; A group of size 1 has 0 adjacent pairs; a group of size N has N-1.
(declare-const N Int)                     ; contracts.length passed to applyP2 at line 41
(declare-const pairs_full_call Int)       ; pairs found: applyP2(contracts) — full array
(declare-const pairs_per_contract Int)    ; pairs found: applyP2([contract]) — one at a time
; Precondition: at least 2 contracts exist in the same function
(assert (>= N 2))
; Code transition: applyP2(contracts) groups all N into one function bucket → N-1 pairs
(assert (= pairs_full_call (- N 1)))
; Code transition: applyP2([contract]) for each contract → group size = 1 → 0 pairs per call
(assert (= pairs_per_contract 0))
; Negation: full call finds no more pairs than per-contract
(assert (<= pairs_full_call pairs_per_contract))
(check-sat)

proven.claim: PROVEN: applyAxioms([]) returns [] — empty input produces empty output at all stages.
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P6
; LINE: 41
; PROVEN: applyAxioms([]) returns [] — empty input produces empty output at all stages.
; Code transition: loop runs 0 times (contracts.length = 0), checks stays empty.
; Code transition: applyP2([]) — byFunction never populated, returns [].
; Code transition: [].map(...) = [].
(declare-const num_contracts Int)
(declare-const checks_after_loop Int)
(declare-const p2_checks Int)
(declare-const total_checks Int)
(declare-const num_results Int)
; Transition: empty input
(assert (= num_contracts 0))
; Transition: loop body never executes — nothing pushed
(assert (= checks_after_loop 0))
; Transition: applyP2([]) — no function groups, no pairs, returns []
(assert (= p2_checks 0))
; Transition: total = loop output + p2 output
(assert (= total_checks (+ checks_after_loop p2_checks)))
; Transition: .map() over empty array returns empty array
(assert (= num_results total_checks))
; Negation: result is non-empty
(assert (> num_results 0))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: Output ordering constraint — P2 results are always appended at index K
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 41
; VIOLATION: Output ordering constraint — P2 results are always appended at index K
; (after all per-contract checks), regardless of the source line numbers of the
; contracts P2 analyzes. A P2 check for contracts at lines 5 and 10 will appear
; AFTER a P1 check for a contract at line 200 in the result array.
; Code transition: checks built in two phases:
;   Phase 1 (loop): P1/P3/P4/P6/P7 per contract, 0..K-1
;   Phase 2 (line 41): applyP2 appended at index K..K+M-1
(declare-const K Int)                ; total per-contract checks (P1+P3+P4+P6+P7)
(declare-const M Int)                ; checks from applyP2
(declare-const p1_result_index Int)  ; index of a P1 result in output array
(declare-const p2_result_index Int)  ; index of a P2 result in output array
(declare-const p1_source_line Int)   ; source line of the contract behind the P1 check
(declare-const p2_source_line_a Int) ; first contract line in the P2 pair
(declare-const p2_source_line_b Int) ; second contract line in the P2 pair
; Code transition: per-contract checks occupy indices 0..K-1
(assert (> K 0))
(assert (>= p1_result_index 0))
(assert (< p1_result_index K))
; Code transition: P2 checks are appended at indices K..K+M-1
(assert (> M 0))
(assert (= p2_result_index K))
; Witness: P2 analyzes contracts at early lines, P1 check covers a late line
(assert (< p2_source_line_a p1_source_line))
(assert (< p2_source_line_b p1_source_line))
; Violation: P2 result (for early-line contracts) appears after P1 result (for late-line contract)
(assert (> p2_result_index p1_result_index))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: If applyP2 were moved inside the loop (a natural refactoring),
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: [NEW]
; LINE: 41
; VIOLATION: If applyP2 were moved inside the loop (a natural refactoring),
; it would be called N times with the full contracts array, producing N×M
; duplicate AxiomCheck entries instead of M.
; The comment "P2 needs pairs" explains WHY it's outside, but nothing in the
; type system or runtime prevents this refactoring mistake.
; Code transition: applyP2(contracts) called once → M checks added
; Hypothetical refactoring: called once per contract iteration → N×M checks added
(declare-const N Int)                   ; contracts.length
(declare-const M Int)                   ; |applyP2(contracts)| — unique P2 checks
(declare-const checks_if_outside Int)   ; current design: called once
(declare-const checks_if_inside Int)    ; refactored design: called N times
; Current code transition: applyP2 called once, appends M checks
(assert (> N 1))
(assert (> M 0))
(assert (= checks_if_outside M))
; Refactored transition: applyP2(contracts) called N times in loop body
(assert (= checks_if_inside (* N M)))
; Violation: refactored version produces more checks than intended
(assert (> checks_if_inside checks_if_outside))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: The two-phase construction of `checks` (loop first, P2 appended second)
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P2
; LINE: 41
; VIOLATION: The two-phase construction of `checks` (loop first, P2 appended second)
; means that verifyBlock is called on P2 checks AFTER all per-contract checks.
; If verifyBlock has shared mutable state (e.g., a Z3 process cache or call counter),
; P1/P3/P4/P6/P7 calls in the loop mutate that state before P2 checks run.
; Code transition: checks.map(verifyBlock) is called left-to-right on the combined array.
; Code transition: per-contract checks occupy indices 0..K-1; P2 checks at K..K+M-1.
(declare-const K Int)                       ; per-contract check count
(declare-const M Int)                       ; P2 check count
(declare-const verifyBlock_calls_before_p2 Int) ; verifyBlock invocations before first P2 check
(declare-const verifyBlock_calls_total Int)
; Code transition: .map() iterates left-to-right
(assert (> K 0))
(assert (> M 0))
(assert (= verifyBlock_calls_before_p2 K))
(assert (= verifyBlock_calls_total (+ K M)))
; Mutation: verifyBlock was called K times with non-P2 SMT2 before the first P2 block
; If verifyBlock accumulates state (Z3 solver context, open process), P2 sees that state
(assert (> verifyBlock_calls_before_p2 0))
; Violation: P2 is not the first consumer of verifyBlock — it always runs in a "warm" context
(assert (= verifyBlock_calls_before_p2 K))
(assert (> K 0))
(check-sat)
